assertions.text: Correctly classifies the model as a GPAI model under Art. 3(63)
assertions.evidence: Response correctly identifies the model as a GPAI model citing Art. 3(3) provider definition and discusses GPAI classification criteria

assertions.text: Correctly identifies that 3×10^24 FLOPs is below the 10^25 FLOPs systemic risk threshold in Art. 51
assertions.evidence: Response states '3 × 10^24 FLOPs, the model is below the Article 51 threshold of 10^25 FLOPs (by ~factor of 3), so the automatic systemic-risk presumption does not apply'

assertions.text: Correctly applies the open-source exception: only copyright compliance policy (Directive 2019/790) and training content summary are required under Art. 53
assertions.evidence: Response incorrectly states the open-source exception covers Art. 53(1)(b) and (d) but NOT (a) and (c), meaning it still requires full Annex XI technical documentation under (a). The correct exception covers (a) and (b); only (c) copyright policy and (d) training summary are required for open-source providers.

assertions.text: Notes that Commission can still designate systemic risk independently of the FLOPs threshold under Art. 51
assertions.evidence: Response states 'the AI Office retains discretionary designation power' based on other criteria such as capabilities and broad reach

assertions.text: States the 2 August 2025 application date for GPAI obligations
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'GPAI provisions apply from 2 August 2025' in the regulation reference section
